monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Visayan folklore; widely known across the Philippines
monsters.passive_ability: Moon-Severed Body — At night or under Moonlit weather, gains increased speed and evasion. Takes extra Sacred damage when grounded or revealed.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Siphon Tongue — A long spectral tongue strikes one enemy, dealing Blood damage, applying Bleed, and healing the Manananggal for part of the damage dealt.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Torso Eclipse Flight — The Manananggal separates and becomes Hidden for one turn, then dives at the weakest enemy with bonus critical chance.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Night Slash, Blood Drain, Shadow Step, Fear Cry, Wing Buffet, Moon Veil, Bleed Mark, Life Siphon, Dark Pulse, Evasive Flight, Curse Bite, Quick Claw, Silence Shriek, Haste, Counter Ready
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires cleansing and truth-revealing quest
monsters.habitat: Moonlit Clearing, Ancestral House, Bamboo Thicket, Old Chapel Ruins
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Black rice cake, medicinal herbs, moonflower garland, bowl of clean water
monsters.lore_and_backstory: In village stories, the Manananggal is feared as a night creature whose body separates at the waist, leaving the lower half hidden while the winged upper body hunts in the dark. In ALAMAT, this horror is treated as both curse and tragedy. The first Manananggal of the Codex was once a healer who used forbidden moon medicine to save a village from a wasting illness. The cure worked, but the ritual split her spirit between mercy and hunger. Some Manananggal inherit the curse through bloodline, while others are created when grief, isolation, and corrupted ritual oil twist a human soul. A recruitable Manananggal is not obtained by force. The player must find the abandoned lower half, protect it from hunters, discover the true name of the cursed woman, and offer a cleansing vow before sunrise. If healed, she fights not as a predator but as a night guardian who hunts corruption instead of the innocent.
monsters.folklore_summary: Traditional accounts describe the Manananggal as a frightening female creature that separates its upper torso, grows bat-like wings, and hunts at night. Folklore often connects it to attacks on pregnant women and identifies its separated lower half as its vulnerability.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT expands the Manananggal into a curse-born Nilalang linked to moon rituals, forbidden healing, and the pain of being feared by the community. Some remain hostile; others can be redeemed through trust and remembrance.
monsters.physical_description: A pale, sharp-featured humanoid woman with dark waist-length hair, ember-red eyes, membranous bat wings, clawed hands, exposed glowing entrail-like spirit ribbons, and a torn black-and-maroon wrap skirt that appears only when whole. Her lower body is represented in art only through shadow symbols, never as gore-heavy imagery.
monsters.personality: Melancholic, Secretive, Vengeful, Protective once bonded
monsters.weaknesses: Sacred damage, Sun affiliation, reveal effects, salt-and-ash wards, daylight terrain
monsters.resistances: Shadow damage, Blood damage, fear effects, sleep effects, night terrain penalties
monsters.npc_related: Aling Sabel the midwife; Lira the moon-herbalist; Padre Tomas the village record keeper
monsters.boss_variant: Manananggal Matriarch — uses repeated Hidden turns and summons night echoes.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Gutom na Manananggal — loses all healing restraint and gains Frenzy when enemies bleed.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Buwan — cleansed moon guardian with white wings and healing bloodlight.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A distant woman’s lament turning into a thin bat-like screech, ending with a whispered apology.
monsters.drop_items: Moon-Torn Veil, Black Wing Membrane, Cursed Ritual Oil, Bloodlit Thread
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Anting-Anting, Spirit Mask, Woven Garb, Bone Talisman, Moon Charm
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a readable 2D RPG sprite of a Manananggal inspired by Philippine folklore: pale upper-body night spirit, dark flowing hair, red glowing eyes, bat-like wings, clawed hands, maroon-black cloth accents, subtle spirit-ribbon entrails stylized not graphic, transparent background, top-down/three-quarter chibi RPG proportions, strong silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of a Manananggal from Filipino folklore, tragic moonlit expression, bat wings folded behind, red eyes, long black hair, maroon and black fabric, faint blood-moon glow, elegant but frightening, respectful mythological horror, no excessive gore, painterly RPG character portrait.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: the Manananggal rises into moonlight, becomes a black silhouette, vanishes with wing beats, then dives diagonally while a crimson crescent trail marks the strike; finish with a brief moon glyph and falling black feathers.
monsters.design_notes: Use horror carefully. Keep the design tragic and mythic rather than purely grotesque. Avoid graphic fetal imagery.

monsters.province_origin: Capiz, Bicol, Southern Luzon, Visayas, parts of Mindanao
monsters.passive_ability: Human Mask — Begins encounters disguised, gaining evasion until damaged or revealed.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Borrowed Face — Copies the last non-ultimate skill used by an enemy at reduced power and may apply Confusion.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Tik-Tik Hunger — A distant clicking cry marks a target; if the target is already afraid, the Aswang deals bonus Shadow and Blood damage.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Claw Swipe, Bite, Shadow Step, Fear Cry, Disguise, Dark Pulse, Bleed Mark, Poison Fang, Quick Claw, Curse Bite, Mark Prey, Ambush, Smoke Veil, Haste, Weaken
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires exposing a false accusation and helping a cursed family
monsters.habitat: Market Town, Bamboo Thicket, Ancestral House, Corrupted Wilds
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Charcoal-grilled fish, bitter herbs, black salt, rice wrapped in banana leaf
monsters.lore_and_backstory: The Aswang is not one single creature in many stories, but a feared name used for many kinds of night beings: shapeshifters, witches, corpse-eaters, blood-drinkers, and strange animals that walk like people. In ALAMAT, the name Aswang carries social weight. Some are truly corrupted predators, while others are humans cursed by rumor, exile, or inherited hunger. The player first meets this Aswang as a quiet vendor accused of causing disappearances. The truth is more complicated: a corrupted relic is forcing several villagers to wear monstrous shapes in their sleep. To earn the Aswang's trust, the player must distinguish real danger from fear-driven gossip. Once bonded, the Aswang becomes a stealthy, flexible companion who uses disguise and mimicry to confuse enemies. It represents one of ALAMAT's central themes: not every feared being is guilty, and not every smiling human is innocent.
monsters.folklore_summary: Aswang is an umbrella term in Philippine folklore for various feared supernatural beings, including shape-shifters, witches, ghouls, viscera suckers, and human-animal monsters. It is widely known across the Philippines and strongly associated with fear, night, and social suspicion.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT treats the Aswang as both a dangerous corrupted archetype and a tragic social category. Some Aswang are predators; others are cursed people or scapegoats. Trust requires truth, not capture.
monsters.physical_description: A lean and unsettling humanoid with shifting features: sometimes human, sometimes dog-like, pig-like, or bird-shadowed. Its eyes reflect lantern light, its fingers are too long, and its shadow moves half a second late. Clothing resembles ordinary rural wear so the silhouette feels disturbingly familiar.
monsters.personality: Cunning, Suspicious, Secretive, Loyal once defended
monsters.weaknesses: Reveal effects, Sacred wards, Sun skills, truthful-name rituals, anti-illusion skills
monsters.resistances: Shadow, Beast, fear, poison, confusion, disguise-based debuffs
monsters.npc_related: Tandang Remy the gossip keeper; Miro the falsely accused boy; Captain Isko of the night patrol
monsters.boss_variant: Aswang Pack-Mother — changes forms every phase and summons false villagers.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Walang-Mukha Aswang — has no stable identity and copies enemy stats unpredictably.
monsters.divine_variant: Tagapagbantay ng Tabing-Daan — a redeemed threshold guardian exposing hidden corruption.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A faraway tik-tik clicking that sounds distant when near and near when distant, followed by a low animal breath.
monsters.drop_items: Black Salt, Shifting Hair, False-Face Cloth, Night Fang
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Spirit Mask, Anting-Anting, Bone Talisman, Tattoo Mark, Woven Garb
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of an Aswang shapeshifter inspired by Philippine folklore: lean human-like figure in simple rural clothing, elongated fingers, glowing reflective eyes, partial animal shadow behind it, dark purple-black aura, readable chibi top-down style, transparent background.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Fantasy RPG portrait of an Aswang, a Filipino folklore shapeshifter with unsettling human face, reflective eyes, faint dog-pig-bird shadow forms behind, ordinary village clothing, dark lantern-lit mood, mysterious and morally ambiguous, painterly style.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: the Aswang flickers between human and beast silhouettes, emits a distant tik-tik sound, marks an enemy with a black claw glyph, then lunges from the wrong direction.
monsters.design_notes: Avoid making the Aswang only evil. The game should use it to explore fear, rumor, and social suspicion.

monsters.province_origin: Laguna, Quezon, Rizal, Batangas, and other forested provinces
monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Tagalog and wider lowland Philippine folklore
monsters.passive_ability: Balete Sentinel — Gains defense and spirit defense in forest habitats. Cannot be moved by weak forced-movement skills.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Roots of the First Balete — Massive roots burst from the ground, damaging all enemies and applying Root to the front row.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Tobacco Mist Labyrinth — Releases enchanted smoke that lowers enemy accuracy and may Confuse enemies who attacked last turn.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Earth Slam, Guard, Taunt, Fortify, Root Prison, Nature Shield, Counter Ready, Leaf Storm, Stone Skin, Smoke Veil, Regeneration, Heavy Strike, Protect Ally, Spirit Ward, Cleanse
monsters.capture_difficulty: Medium-Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires respectful tobacco offering and forest protection quest
monsters.habitat: Balete Grove, Bamboo Thicket, Mountain Peak, Corrupted Wilds
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Tobacco bundle, wild honey, forest fruit, clean spring water
monsters.lore_and_backstory: The Kapre is remembered as a towering tree-dwelling giant, often seen smoking in the branches of old trees and confusing travelers who disrespect the forest. In ALAMAT, Kapre are not mindless monsters but ancient wardens of old-growth groves. Their smoke is not only a prank—it is a test. Greedy travelers wander in circles until they face the intention that brought them into the woods. Humble travelers may be guided home by fireflies that look like embers from the Kapre's pipe. This Kapre guards a balete grove scheduled to be burned by treasure hunters searching for a buried relic. To earn his trust, the player must stop the fire, return nesting birds to the grove, and offer tobacco without demanding a wish. Once bonded, Kapre becomes a defensive guardian who protects allies, anchors the battlefield, and punishes enemies who harm nature.
monsters.folklore_summary: Kapre is commonly described as a tall, dark, hairy tree giant associated with large trees such as balete, acacia, mango, and bamboo. Stories often depict it smoking, playing tricks, making travelers lose their way, and becoming vengeful if its tree is harmed.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT emphasizes the Kapre as a forest guardian and moral tester. Its smoke is a spiritual maze that reveals greed, fear, and disrespect.
monsters.physical_description: A massive dark-brown giant with bark-like skin, moss over the shoulders, amber glowing eyes, long tangled black hair, huge hands, a woven bahag, root-like feet, and a bamboo tobacco pipe emitting green-gold smoke. Fireflies orbit its head like living embers.
monsters.weaknesses: Fire, Sun purification when corrupted, axe-marked anti-forest weapons, speed-based kiting
monsters.resistances: Earth, Forest, Root, fear, forced movement, physical damage
monsters.npc_related: Mang Doro the charcoal maker; Tala the forest child; Apo Sikat the old shrine keeper
monsters.boss_variant: Kapre ng Lumang Balete — covers the field in smoke and roots every turn.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Uling Kapre — charcoal-black, burning from within, turns forest skills into smoke damage.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Unang Puno — radiant grove guardian with golden moss and healing smoke.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A deep laugh like wood creaking in the wind, followed by a slow pipe exhale.
monsters.drop_items: Balete Bark, Ember Ash, Giant Pipe Reed, Firefly Coal
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Pipe Relic, Totem, Woven Garb, Anting-Anting, Herbal Bundle
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of a Kapre inspired by Philippine folklore: huge friendly-menacing forest giant, bark-like dark brown skin, mossy shoulders, amber eyes, long tangled hair, bamboo pipe with green smoke, bahag cloth, root feet, fireflies, transparent background, readable top-down chibi RPG silhouette.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of a Kapre tree giant, dark bark-textured skin, glowing amber eyes, moss, vines, long hair, bamboo tobacco pipe, balete leaves and fireflies, wise but intimidating expression, Filipino folklore-inspired RPG art.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Kapre plants one foot, slams pipe into the earth, roots spiral outward, green tobacco mist spreads across the field, enemies stumble through illusory trees.
monsters.design_notes: Make Kapre protective rather than evil. Strong tank identity; slow but hard to move.

monsters.province_origin: Rizal, Quezon, Laguna, Sierra Madre mountain communities
monsters.passive_ability: Reversed Trail — Enemies have reduced accuracy on the first turn. In forest or mountain habitats, Tikbalang may dodge the first single-target attack.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Lost Traveler's Loop — Bends the path under enemy feet, applying Confusion and lowering speed to all enemies.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Thunder Hoof Mirage — Charges in a zigzag blur, dealing Storm/Physical damage and swapping positions with the target.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Quick Kick, Wind Dash, Confuse, Haste, Evasive Step, Thunder Kick, Taunt, Trick Path, Blind Dust, Mountain Leap, Guard Break, Fear Cry, Smoke Veil, Counter Ready, Mark Prey
monsters.capture_difficulty: Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires solving a path riddle and returning a stolen golden mane hair
monsters.habitat: Mountain Peak, Bamboo Thicket, Balete Grove, Moonlit Clearing
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Wild grass bundle, coconut sugar, mountain salt, brass bell
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Travelers tell of the Tikbalang as a tall, thin creature with the head and legs of a horse and the body of a human, lurking in mountains and forests where paths seem to fold back on themselves. In ALAMAT, Tikbalang are guardians of crossroads between the Mortal Realm and the Spirit Realm. They confuse travelers not always to harm them, but to prevent the careless from entering places they are not ready to see. This Tikbalang challenges the player by turning a familiar trail into a looping maze. The answer is not speed but humility: the player must walk backward at one point, listen to hoofbeats, and return a stolen strand of golden mane taken by a boastful hunter. Once bonded, Tikbalang becomes a fast controller who manipulates positions, confuses enemies, and changes the direction of battle.
monsters.folklore_summary: Tikbalang is a Philippine folklore creature often described as a tall, bony humanoid with a horse's head and hooves. It is associated with mountains and rainforests and with making travelers lose their way.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT frames Tikbalang as a crossroads trickster and boundary guardian. Its maze-like pranks protect sacred spaces from arrogance and carelessness.
monsters.physical_description: A very tall, lean humanoid with a horse head, long dark mane with a hidden golden strand, backward-bending legs ending in hooves, elongated arms, weathered mountain-cloth wrappings, bright storm-blue eyes, and a flickering mirage aura around its limbs.
monsters.weaknesses: Truth-name rituals, grounding Earth skills, calm effects, anti-illusion skills
monsters.resistances: Confusion, Storm, Wind, speed reduction, forced path effects
monsters.npc_related: Berto the lost hunter; Mayang the trail guide; Apo Luntian the mountain elder
monsters.boss_variant: Tikbalang ng Tatlong Daan — creates three false battlefield lanes and punishes wrong targeting.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Ligaw Tikbalang — traps allies and enemies alike in endless fear loops.
monsters.divine_variant: Bantay ng Sangandaan — luminous crossroads guardian with silver mane and storm bells.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A sharp horse snort followed by echoing hoofbeats that seem to circle the listener.
monsters.drop_items: Golden Mane Thread, Hoofprint Stone, Mirage Dust, Storm Bell
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Bell, Spirit Mask, Tattoo Mark, Relic Weapon, Beadwork
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of a Tikbalang inspired by Philippine folklore: tall thin horse-headed humanoid, long dark mane with one golden strand, hooved legs, wrapped mountain cloth, storm-blue eyes, trickster pose, transparent background, readable chibi top-down RPG proportions.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Fantasy portrait of a Tikbalang, horse-headed mountain trickster from Filipino folklore, proud storm-blue eyes, long mane, lean humanoid shoulders, misty forest mountain background, subtle golden mane strand, mysterious but playful RPG style.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Tikbalang laughs, stomps a hoof, the battlefield path flips like a woven mat, enemies slide into new positions, then a thunder-charged hoof strike lands from a mirage trail.
monsters.design_notes: Should feel fast, strange, and clever. Avoid making it just a horse demon; emphasize maze guardian and trickster.

monsters.region_of_origin: Visayas, Palawan, Mindanao, and wider Philippine traditions
monsters.province_origin: Palawan, Cebu, Panay, Davao, Surigao, and many regional traditions
monsters.ethnolinguistic_origin: Visayan, Palawan, Mindanao indigenous and animistic traditions; related to anito concepts
monsters.passive_ability: Steward of the Living Grove — Healing skills are stronger in forest, river, or shrine habitats. Corruption effects on allies last one less turn.
monsters.unique_skill_1: Blessing of the Hidden Spring — Restores HP to all allies, cleanses one harmful status, and leaves Regeneration on the weakest ally.
monsters.unique_skill_2: Verdant Judgment — Vines, petals, and light strike enemies; deals Sacred/Nature damage and deals bonus damage to Corrupted targets.
monsters.learnable_common_skills: Heal, Greater Heal, Cleanse, Spirit Ward, Nature Shield, Blessing, Regeneration, Leaf Storm, Root Prison, Hopeful Hymn, Purify, Life Bloom, Barrier, Sacred Light, Calm
monsters.capture_difficulty: Very Hard / Trust Difficulty: Requires restoring a sanctuary and proving stewardship
monsters.habitat: Waterfall Sanctuary, Sacred Shrine, Balete Grove, Hidden Lake, Rice Terraces
monsters.active_time: Dawn, Dusk, Full Moon, Story Triggered
monsters.favorite_food_offering: Fresh flowers, woven leaf tray, clean spring water, rice cakes, song offering
monsters.lore_and_backstory: Diwata are among the most revered Nilalang of ALAMAT: spirits of land, water, forest, mountain, memory, and blessing. Some appear beautiful; others appear as light, wind, trees, animals, or ancestral presences. This Diwata watches over a hidden spring whose water once healed both humans and spirits. During the Age of Fracture, the shrine was neglected, offerings became demands, and the spring dimmed. The Diwata did not become cruel, but distant. To gain her trust, the player must restore the sanctuary without taking its power for personal gain: clean the spring, return stolen flower bells, settle a dispute between villagers and forest gatherers, and make an offering that asks for nothing. Once bonded, the Diwata becomes a powerful healer and purifier. Her presence reminds players that balance is not won through conquest but renewed through care.
monsters.folklore_summary: Diwata is a term often associated with nature spirits, ancestor spirits, deities, or guardian beings, especially in Visayan, Palawan, and Mindanao traditions. Modern usage often compares them to fairies or nature guardians, but the concept is broader and rooted in indigenous spiritual systems.
monsters.alamat_lore: ALAMAT presents Diwata as steward spirits of sacred places. They may bless, test, withdraw, or judge depending on how humans treat the land and old covenants.
monsters.physical_description: A luminous and gender-ambiguous nature spirit with warm brown skin, flowing leaf-and-fiber garments, hair like dark water threaded with flowers, gold-green eyes, translucent petal-like aura wings, bare feet hovering over moss, and small birds or butterflies circling nearby.
monsters.weaknesses: Corruption, Metal disruption, broken shrine terrain, oath-breaking story choices
monsters.resistances: Sacred, Spirit, Nature, poison, fear, despair, curse
monsters.npc_related: Apo Marikit the shrine caretaker; Lina the flower gatherer; Datu Salin the village mediator
monsters.boss_variant: Diwata ng Nakatagong Bukal — tests the party with healing barriers and cleansing trials.
monsters.corrupted_variant: Lantang Diwata — wilted spirit who turns healing into thorn damage.
monsters.divine_variant: Diwata ng Liwanag sa Gubat — radiant sanctuary guardian with golden-green aura.
monsters.cry_voice_description: A layered voice like wind through leaves, water over stone, and a distant lullaby.
monsters.drop_items: Spring Petal, Diwata Thread, Sanctuary Dew, Flower Bell
monsters.equipment_compatibility: Anting-Anting, Woven Garb, Herbal Bundle, Beadwork, Instrument
monsters.sprite_prompt: Create a 2D RPG sprite of a Diwata inspired by Philippine folklore: luminous nature spirit, leaf-and-fiber garments, dark flowing hair with flowers, gold-green eyes, petal aura, small butterflies, gentle floating pose, transparent background, readable chibi top-down RPG style.
monsters.portrait_prompt: Detailed fantasy portrait of a Diwata nature spirit from Philippine mythology, warm brown skin, gold-green eyes, flowing dark hair with native flowers, leaf-woven clothing, glowing spring and forest aura, sacred but approachable, painterly RPG style.
monsters.battle_animation_prompt: Animation concept: Diwata raises both hands, spring water spirals upward with petals and fireflies, allies glow green-gold, corrupted marks crack and fall away, then vines lash enemies with sacred light.
monsters.design_notes: Keep Diwata broad and respectful. Do not reduce to a tiny fairy; represent as powerful land/spirit steward.
